Over the next two weeks, we bring you a two-part program about the artist, first broadcast on Awaye in 2006. Today, the first installment. It's not so much about Namatjira himself, but the unexpected visit he made in the 1950s to the Cootamundra Aboriginal Girls Training Home in western New South Wales. It's the story of a painting and what it came to represent. Namatjira's imagery, typically of ghost gums and hidden valleys in Central Australia, captured the popular imagination of the 1950s. Mass-produced prints of his landscapes could be seen hanging on the walls of many white suburban homes. But an original hung in the.
